authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams (Mouth) layer record for gaz_id 269264, "Wolf Creek", Stream, Mono County, CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":269264,"gaz_name":"Wolf Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Mono"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13306904.7409, 4628700.2675) converts to lon -119.537946, lat 38.347970, matching HomeTownLocator's listed coordinate for this feature (38.3479695, -119.5379463) to within ~10m; the second multipoint vertex (-119.620291, 38.354727) is ~6.3km further along the creek. The file's previous coordinate (-119.6100, 38.3350) was ~6.4km away from the GNIS-recorded point and has been corrected.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(167) — Wolf Creek and tributaries (Mono Co.)
sources.quotedText: (167) Wolf Creek and tributaries (tributary to West Walker River) (Mono Co.). All year. Only artificial flies with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: Wolf Creek Fishery Management Guidelines (CDFW Heritage and Wild Trout Program, Region 6, 2025)
sources.quotedText: Wolf Creek was designated as a Heritage Trout Water by the California Fish and Game Commission in 2020. It supports a restored, self-sustaining population of Walker-strain Lahontan Cutthroat Trout (LCT) within its native range of the Walker Basin.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (updated June 1, 2026)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.note: Open all year, catch-and-release only (0 trout bag limit). Wolf Creek is a designated Heritage Trout Water managed for its restored, self-sustaining Walker-strain Lahontan cutthroat trout population.

regulations.humanSummary: Wolf Creek and tributaries (tributary to West Walker River) open all year, catch-and-release only
regulations.verbatimText: Wolf Creek and tributaries (tributary to West Walker River) (Mono Co.). All year.

parameters.note: Only artificial flies with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial flies with barbless hooks may be used
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial flies with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: 0 trout — catch-and-release only, no trout may be kept.

regulations.humanSummary: Zero trout bag limit — catch-and-release only, to protect the recovering native Lahontan cutthroat trout population

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
